insight:
Yesterday was a solid day with your 5.1km run showing good pacing at 30 minutes and that 160 bpm average heart rate indicates you're working at a productive aerobic intensity. Your 45 bpm resting heart rate and low stress level of 21/100 demonstrate excellent cardiovascular fitness and recovery status heading into race week.

Your HRV of 112ms sits perfectly within your baseline range of 93-116ms, which means your nervous system is well-recovered and ready for training. Combined with 7.4 hours of quality sleep, you're green-lighted to proceed with today's planned session without any modifications - your body is handling the training load beautifully.

With 2,729 calories burned yesterday against your 2,300 target, you created a 429-calorie deficit. While this supports your 80-85kg goal, don't restrict too aggressively this close to race day. Focus on hitting that 175g protein target today to support muscle recovery and maintain your energy for Sunday's half marathon.
